title: bubonium graveolens subsp. odorum
intro: Bubonium graveolens subsp. odorum, also known as Sea Lavender, is a subspecies of the Bubonium graveolens plant, a perennial herb native to the coastal regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Sea Lavender include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Sea Lavender include its ability to reduce anxiety, promote relaxation, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia, as well as its antimicrobial properties which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Sea Lavender is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote a good night's sleep, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
